content.claim: By 2024, more than 1,200 of 3,155 Hungarian settlements had no public educational institutions, and primary education sites fell from 3,609 to 3,521 between 2021 and 2024, indicating a contracting feeder network from which 5th/7th-grade selection cohorts must be drawn.
content.context: Fresh live research through the School network planning lens
limitations: Data is aggregate across all primary schools, not disaggregated by school type or gimnázium feed-rate
Causal drivers of closure (demographic decline, consolidation policy, resource allocation) are not specified
Impact on 6-/8-year track recruitment specifically is inferred, not directly measured
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
